name: Shoes of Peace
description: The 'Shoes of Peace', also known as the 'Preparation of the Gospel of Peace', are a metaphorical component of the Armor of God described in Ephesians 6:15. These shoes represent the readiness and willingness of believers to share the Gospel of Jesus Christ, bringing a message of reconciliation and peace with God to the world. They are not literal shoes, but rather symbolize the firm foundation and readiness to stand firm in faith and proclaim the good news of salvation. The shoes empower the wearer to traverse difficult paths and overcome obstacles in spreading the message of peace. They offer stability and protection against the spiritual forces of evil, allowing the believer to confidently and effectively communicate the Gospel. The preparation for the Gospel of Peace implies a constant state of readiness, a commitment to knowing and understanding the message of peace, and a willingness to share it with others, regardless of the circumstances. The significance lies in the active role believers play in extending God's peace to a broken world, embodying the love and grace of Christ in their interactions and actions. Wearing these shoes means actively pursuing reconciliation and justice, reflecting the transformative power of the Gospel in all aspects of life. They are a symbol of hope and a call to action, urging believers to be ambassadors of peace in a world often marked by conflict and division.
subtitle: Readiness to share the Gospel
currentLocation: Within believers who actively share the Gospel

keyMyths.title: The Armor of God
keyMyths.description: Ephesians 6:10-18 describes the full armor of God, including the Shoes of Peace, as essential for spiritual warfare against evil.

keyMyths.title: The Great Commission
keyMyths.description: Jesus commands his followers to go and make disciples of all nations, embodying the active spreading of the Gospel of Peace.

sources.author: Paul the Apostle
sources.description: Describes the Armor of God, including the Shoes of Peace.
sources.text: Describes the Armor of God, including the Shoes of Peace.
